title: Topological Versions of the Rokhlin Lemma
speaker: Petr Naryshkin
abstract:
The classical Rokhlin Lemma asserts that for an aperiodic measure-preserving transformation $T$ of a probability space, one can find a "tower" of sets on which $T$ acts by translation and which covers almost all of the space. This result is a basic tool in ergodic theory and plays a crucial role in the proofs of several fundamental theorems (e.g., Dye's and Ornstein's). In the 1970s, Ornstein and Weiss extended the Rokhlin Lemma to free actions of discrete amenable groups, thus allowing many of these results to be generalized beyond the $\mathbb{Z}$-action setting.
It is natural to ask for analogous statements in topological dynamics, where an amenable group acts by homeomorphisms on a compact metrizable space. Indeed, such results proved to be extremely useful in certain topics, such as the study of C$^*$-crossed products and the theory of mean dimension. However, it turns out that the situation is significantly more subtle than in the measurable case. On the one hand, there are several different ways to formulate a topological analogue of the Ornstein–Weiss theorem, useful for different problems. On the other hand, unlike in the measurable setting, genuine difficulties arise, stemming both from the algebraic structure of the group and from the topology of the space.
In this talk, I will begin by reviewing the classical measurable results and introducing the corresponding topological formulations. I will then briefly discuss some applications (including my work on shift embeddability), and conclude by outlining the conditions under which these topological Rokhlin-type properties can be established (following, among others, work with Petrakos).
tags: Joint IAS/PU Groups and Dynamics Seminar

proof_stages.label: Measurable Rokhlin towers and amenable-group castles
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins with the classical measurable Rokhlin lemma for aperiodic probability-measure-preserving Z-actions and then formulates the Ornstein-Weiss amenable-group version using Følner-like shapes, measurable bases, and a small-measure remainder.

proof_stages.label: Topological reformulation and zero-dimensional clopen castles
proof_stages.summary: The setting is changed to free actions of amenable groups on compact metrizable spaces, and in the zero-dimensional case the measurable castle statement is upgraded to clopen castles whose remainders are uniformly small for all invariant probability measures.

proof_stages.label: Absorbing the remainder through comparison
proof_stages.summary: A Følner-stability observation shows that small extra tower levels do not destroy invariance, motivating the comparison property as exactly the combinatorial mechanism for cutting and translating the remainder into the towers; in the zero-dimensional setting, exact clopen castles are equivalent to comparison.

proof_stages.label: Uniform Rokhlin property beyond zero dimension
proof_stages.summary: For general compact spaces the clopen condition is replaced by open castles with uniformly small remainder, defining the uniform Rokhlin property, and the combination of this property with comparison is identified as the key topological substitute for the measurable Rokhlin lemma.

proof_stages.label: Crossed-product C*-algebra application
proof_stages.summary: For a dynamical system one forms a crossed-product C*-algebra, and Rokhlin castles produce finite-dimensional matrix subalgebras whose units cover the whole algebraic unit only when the remainder is controlled or empty, leading to regularity/classification consequences such as Z-stability under additional hypotheses.

proof_stages.label: Mean dimension and shift embeddings
proof_stages.summary: Mean dimension is sketched via metrics over Følner sets, Rokhlin castles are used to build simplicial approximations that retain partial dynamics, and this feeds a theorem embedding systems with uniform Rokhlin property, comparison, and mean dimension less than m/2 into the cubical Bernoulli shift.

proof_stages.label: Known group classes with automatic comparison
proof_stages.summary: A large class S of infinite amenable groups is described through growth, direct limits, extensions, large finite normal subgroups, and groups of dynamical origin, and for groups in this class every zero-dimensional free action has comparison, suggesting the conjectural full amenable-group result.

examples.name: Dye's orbit-equivalence theorem
examples.note: Dye's theorem is cited as a classical result where measurable Rokhlin towers are an important ingredient, illustrating the lemma's power rather than generating a new step in this talk.
examples.engagement_reason: He states Dye’s orbit-equivalence theorem as a favorite application of the Rokhlin lemma but does not analyze or prove it.
